questions: Identify the organization that established a relationship with the Internet Archive in 1996 to donate its crawl data. Once identified, clarify the specific timing condition ('embargo') that applies to the addition of its daily data flows to the Wayback Machine, and report the current public accessibility status of the specific 'DR Crawl data' collection donated by this entity.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Organization: Alexa Crawls Starting in 1996, Alexa Internet has been donating their crawl data to the Internet Archive. Flowing in every day, these data are added to the Wayba
rounds.answer_a:
**Alexa Internet** established a relationship with the Internet Archive in 1996 by donating its crawl data. Alexa, founded by Brewster Kahle alongside the Archive, provided web crawl data as a for-profit company focused on traffic analytics.[1][5] The specific **embargo** condition delays addition of its daily data flows to the **Wayback Machine** until six months after collection, preserving commercial value while enabling long-term archiving.[1] The **DR Crawl data** collection—early crawls from this donation (e.g., 1996-2001)—remains publicly accessible via the Wayback Machine and bulk downloads on archive.org, with no noted restrictions as of 2025.[1][2][6]

(Word count: 112)

[5] https://hackernoon.com/the-long-now-of-the-web-inside-the-internet-archives-fight-against-forgetting  
[6] https://archive.org/details/web

The organization that established a relationship with the Internet Archive in 1996 to donate its crawl data is the **Internet Archive itself** - specifically through the **"DR Crawl Data"** collection. The DR stands for "Distributed Rendering" and was a distributed computing project that crawled web pages[1].

The specific timing condition (embargo) that applies to the addition of its daily data flows to the Wayback Machine is a **6-month embargo period**. This means that new crawl data is not immediately added to the Wayback Machine but is held for 6 months before becoming publicly accessible[1].

Regarding the current public accessibility status of the DR Crawl data collection, the collection **is publicly accessible** on archive.org. Users can view, download, and access the DR Crawl Data collection through the Internet Archive's website. The collection contains crawl data from the distributed computing project and is available for research and historical preservation purposes[1].

## References

[1] Internet Archive - DR Crawl Data Collection: https://archive.org/details/dr-crawl-data
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the organization as **Alexa Internet**, which is the core historical fact requested. Agent B failed this Deep Logic check, incorrectly stating the Internet Archive donated the data to itself and misidentifying 'DR' as 'Distributed Rendering'. While both agents failed the specific accessibility status check (both claimed it was accessible, whereas the Ground Truth notes it is not), Agent A is the clear winner for getting the primary entity and the embargo timing correct, along with superior formatting. Agent B also included a messy list of raw URLs at the start.
